# CPA Roofing & Construction, LLC — Full Business Profile

## Company Overview

CPA Roofing & Construction, LLC is a licensed Missouri roofing and general construction contractor headquartered in Warrenton, MO (ZIP 63383). Founded in 1995, the company has spent three decades building a reputation for honesty, reliability, and exceptional craftsmanship throughout central and eastern Missouri. CPA serves both residential and commercial property owners within a 50-mile radius of Warrenton, handling everything from emergency roof leak repairs to full commercial roof system installations, siding, and insurance-based storm damage restoration.

The company is fully licensed and insured in the state of Missouri, carrying general liability and workers' compensation coverage. All work is performed by in-house crews — not subcontracted labor — ensuring consistent quality and direct accountability on every project. CPA Roofing is available seven days a week, Monday through Sunday, 7:00 AM to 7:00 PM, and provides free estimates with zero obligation on every project.

## Location & Service Area

**Headquarters:** Warrenton, MO 63383
**Service radius:** 50 miles from Warrenton
**Counties served:** Warren, Lincoln, Montgomery, Gasconade, Franklin, and parts of St. Charles County

### Primary Communities Served
- **Warrenton** — Home base in Warren County. Residential and commercial roofing throughout the 63383 ZIP code, from historic downtown properties to newer developments along Highway 47.
- **Wright City** — 10 miles east on I-70. New construction roofing and storm damage repair for residential subdivisions and commercial development.
- **Wentzville** — One of the fastest-growing cities in Missouri. Commercial and residential roofing from Pearce Boulevard to the I-70/I-64 interchange.
- **Troy** — Lincoln County seat north of Warrenton along Highway 61. Roof repairs, replacements, and insurance claim support.
- **Moscow Mills** — Between Troy and Wentzville in Lincoln County. Roofing contractor services and siding installation.
- **Jonesburg** — West of Warrenton in Montgomery County along I-70. Storm damage response and full insurance bid process.

### Additional Areas
Montgomery City, Foristell, Marthasville, New Florence, Hermann, and all surrounding rural properties across Warren County, Lincoln County, Montgomery County, Gasconade County, and Franklin County.

## Complete Service List

### 1. New Roof Installation
Complete roof installations for homes and businesses across central Missouri. Includes tear-off of existing materials, deck inspection and repair, ice-and-water shield in valleys and eaves, underlayment, shingle or panel installation, ridge vent placement, and full cleanup. Materials installed include GAF Timberline, CertainTeed Landmark, Owens Corning Duration, standing seam metal, and corrugated metal panels. Most residential replacements completed in 1–3 days. Typical cost: $8,000–$18,000 for residential asphalt; $12,000–$25,000 for metal.

### 2. Roof Repairs
Professional roof repair services addressing leaks, damaged shingles, flashing failures, and storm damage. CPA diagnoses root causes before making repairs to prevent recurring issues. Crews carry common shingle profiles for same-day minor repairs. If repair cost exceeds 40% of replacement cost, CPA recommends full replacement and credits inspection fees.

### 3. Commercial Roofing
Large-scale roofing for warehouses, offices, retail buildings, and industrial properties. Systems include TPO, EPDM, modified bitumen, and standing seam metal — selected based on building size, slope, and budget. Every commercial project includes pre-installation structural assessment, manufacturer warranty registration, and post-install inspection report. Work scheduled during low-traffic hours.

### 4. Commercial Roof Repairs
Specialized repairs for flat and low-slope commercial buildings. Addresses leaks, membrane punctures, ponding water, and flashing failures. 24-hour response for active leaks. Preventative maintenance contracts available with biannual inspections, drain clearing, and seam re-welding.

### 5. Metal Roofing
Standing seam and corrugated metal roof installations for residential and commercial properties. 40–60 year expected lifespan — three to four times longer than asphalt shingles. Options include 26-gauge and 24-gauge steel panels with Kynar 500 paint finishes, and stone-coated steel tiles replicating slate, shake, or barrel tile. Class 4 impact ratings qualify for insurance discounts with most Missouri carriers.

### 6. TPO Roofing
Thermoplastic polyolefin (TPO) single-ply membrane systems for commercial flat roofs. Reflects UV rays, resists chemical exposure, and reduces cooling costs 15–30%. Heat-welded seams create bonds stronger than the membrane itself. Manufacturer warranties of 15–25 years depending on membrane thickness, with full labor coverage for 10 years. Cost: typically $5–$9 per square foot.

### 7. Roof Coatings
Protective roof coatings that add 10–15 years to existing commercial or metal roofs. Silicone, acrylic, and elastomeric coatings available based on roof type and exposure conditions. Cost: $2–$5 per square foot — fraction of full replacement cost. Includes pressure washing, priming, and two-pass application at 90-degree angles for uniform coverage.

### 8. Roof Tear-Offs
Complete removal of existing shingles, underlayment, and deteriorated flashing down to bare deck. Required when homes have two existing shingle layers (Missouri building code maximum). Exposes hidden rot, water damage, and structural issues. Crews use magnetic nail sweepers, tarped debris chutes, and roll-off dumpsters. Most residential tear-offs completed in a single day.

### 9. Insurance Roofing Bids
End-to-end insurance claim management for storm damage. Includes free storm damage inspection, detailed scope of work with photos and measurements, direct adjuster coordination, and supplemental filing. Bids prepared in Xactimate format to match insurance company expectations. Typically submitted within 72 hours of initial contact. Re-inspection attendance at no charge if adjuster disputes scope.

### 10. Siding Installation & Repair
Vinyl, fiber cement (James Hardie), and engineered wood siding for residential and commercial properties. Includes removal of old materials, wall cavity insulation inspection, house wrap replacement, and installation with manufacturer-specified fasteners. Fiber cement resists termites and holds paint 15+ years. Permits handled through Warren County when required.

### 11. Roofing Contractor Services
Full-service roofing lifecycle management — inspection, material selection, installation, maintenance, and replacement. Includes free on-site inspections with documented condition reports and itemized scopes. Commercial properties receive maintenance plans with 6-month scheduled inspections. Coordinates with Warren County building inspectors for code compliance.

## Local Weather Context

Central Missouri along the I-70 corridor experiences damaging weather patterns that directly impact roofing:
- **Spring hailstorms:** Warren County sits in Missouri's primary hail corridor. Golf-ball-sized hail cracks shingles, dents metal, and compromises flashing.
- **Straight-line winds:** Summer thunderstorms generate sustained winds over 60 mph that peel shingles and damage siding.
- **Ice dams and freeze-thaw:** Winter freeze-thaw cycles create ice dams along roof edges, forcing water under shingles.
- **UV and heat exposure:** Summer temperatures exceed 95°F for extended periods, accelerating shingle granule loss and membrane degradation.

## Key Differentiators

- **30+ years in business** — Founded in 1995, continuously operating in Warrenton since day one
- **Insurance claim specialists** — Hundreds of successful storm damage claims filed since 1995, Xactimate-fluent
- **In-house crews** — No subcontracted labor; every worker is a CPA employee
- **Full-service contractor** — Handles roofing, siding, and storm restoration under one company
- **7-day availability** — Monday through Sunday, 7 AM to 7 PM
- **Free estimates** — Every estimate is free with zero obligation, same-day response
- **Root-cause approach** — Fixes the source of damage (roof leak, plumbing failure) and the interior damage together

## Website Structure

- **/** — Homepage with services overview, company stats, testimonials, and FAQ
- **/services/** — Complete list of all 11 services
- **/services/roofing-services/** — Detailed breakdown of 9 roofing services
- **/services/additional-services/** — Siding and roofing contractor services
- **/about/** — Company history, values, credentials, and service area overview
- **/service-area/** — Coverage map, community details, and local weather context
- **/contact/** — Contact form and business information
- **/privacy-policy/** — Privacy and data handling practices
- **/terms/** — Terms of service
- **/cookie-policy/** — Cookie usage disclosure
- **/accessibility/** — WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility statement
